title: hieracium villosum subsp. doratophyllum
intro: Hieracium villosum subsp. doratophyllum, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Hieracium villosum subsp. doratophyllum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
